lessons.title: Definition of a Limit
lessons.content:
Let $f(x)$ be a function defined on an interval that contains $x=a$, except possibly at $x=a$. Then we say that,

$$\lim_{x \to a} f(x) = L$$

if for every number $\epsilon > 0$ there is some number $\delta > 0$ such that $|f(x) - L| < \epsilon$ whenever $0 < |x - a| < \delta$. This is the formal $\epsilon-\delta$ definition of a limit.

lessons.title: Limit Properties
lessons.content:
A fundamental property is that the limit of a sum is the sum of the limits:

$$\lim_{x \to a} [f(x) + g(x)] = \lim_{x \to a} f(x) + \lim_{x \to a} g(x)$$

Also, for products: $\lim_{x \to a} [f(x)g(x)] = \lim_{x \to a} f(x) \cdot \lim_{x \to a} g(x)$.

lessons.title: Definition of the Derivative
lessons.content:
The derivative of $f(x)$ with respect to $x$ is the function $f'(x)$ and is defined as,

$$f'(x) = \lim_{h \to 0} \frac{f(x+h) - f(x)}{h}$$

This represents the slope of the tangent line to the function at any point $x$.

lessons.title: Power Rule & Chain Rule
lessons.content:
The Power Rule for a polynomial $f(x) = x^n$ states that:

$$f'(x) = nx^{n-1}$$

The Chain Rule is incredibly important for composite functions. If $h(x) = f(g(x))$, then:

$$h'(x) = f'(g(x))g'(x)$$

topics.title: Integrals
topics.description: Anti-derivatives and area under the curve.

lessons.title: Definite Integral
lessons.content:
The definite integral of a continuous function $f(x)$ over the interval $[a, b]$ is denoted by:

$$\int_{a}^{b} f(x) dx$$

It represents the signed area between the $x$-axis and the graph of the function.

quizzes.question: What is the value of $\lim_{x \to 2} x^2$?
